description: Add a read-only sub-agent-type warning to /delegate SKILL.md (point-of-use bullet + Key Resources annotation) and a deterministic /eval guard probe, closing the rl-delegation lesson (#57).

userStories.title: Warn at point of use (Worker configuration block)
userStories.description: As an orchestrator reading /delegate while spawning workers, I want the read-only constraint stated in the Worker-configuration block so I pick the right subagent_type before a wave silently no-ops.
acceptanceCriteria: In .claude/skills/delegate/SKILL.md, find the 'Worker configuration:' bullet list under the Execute-waves section (currently has Model and run_in_background bullets) by heading/anchor text, NOT by line number
Add a bullet stating implementer, pm, and critic sub-agent types are read-only (tools: Read, Glob, Grep, Bash) and silently make zero file changes
The bullet is actionable: it tells the reader to set subagent_type: general-purpose (or claude) in the Agent tool call for any worker that must Write/Edit files
After the edit: grep -n 'read-only' .claude/skills/delegate/SKILL.md returns a hit inside the Worker-configuration block, and 'general-purpose' appears in that same bullet
No other section's meaning changed; no .claude/agents/*.md files edited

userStories.title: Annotate the Key Resources table
userStories.description: As a reader scanning the Reference resource table, I want the read-only agent types annotated so the constraint is visible where the agents are listed.
acceptanceCriteria: In the Reference 'Key Resources' table (find by heading text), annotate the Implementer / Critic / PM rows as 'read-only — no Write/Edit' via an inline note appended to the existing Path cell — do NOT add a new column (table is 2-column)

Add a note (new row or a sentence directly under the table) naming general-purpose (or claude) as the type to use for file-writing workers, noting general-purpose is a built-in type with no agent-definition file (do not reference a .claude/agents/general-purpose.md path)
Do NOT modify or fix the existing 'Agent: Council' row (pre-existing stale reference, out of scope)
After the edit: grep -n 'read-only' .claude/skills/delegate/SKILL.md returns a hit in the Reference table region
Table still renders: every row has exactly 2 pipe-delimited cells

userStories.title: Add the guard probe
userStories.description: As the harness eval suite, I want a deterministic probe that verifies the /delegate read-only warning is present so a future edit cannot silently strip it.
acceptanceCriteria: New file evals/probes/rl-delegation-write-worker.sh, executable (chmod +x), mirrors evals/probes/clean-restore.sh convention: shebang '#!/usr/bin/env bash', '# tier: A', '# source:', '# desc:' header, 'set -euo pipefail', ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/../.." && pwd)", all status messages to stderr (>&2)
The '# source:' header uses canonical format: '# source: memory/MEMORY.md 2026-06-10 (rl-delegation) #57'
SKIPPED (exit 2) when .claude/skills/delegate/SKILL.md is absent

The probe scopes its check to the warning region (Worker-configuration block and/or Reference table) using awk/sed to extract the section then grep within it — not a whole-file grep — so an unrelated occurrence cannot cause a false PASS
PASS (exit 0) iff the warning region contains BOTH (a) a 'read-only' warning naming at least one of implementer/pm/critic AND (b) a 'general-purpose' recommendation for write workers
REGRESSION (exit 1) if either element is absent; the message distinguishes 'section/region not found' from 'required phrase missing'
bash evals/probes/rl-delegation-write-worker.sh against the updated skill exits 0 and prints PASS; temporarily stripping the warning makes it exit 1 REGRESSION (spot-check, then revert)

userStories.title: Eval gate green (no new regression)
userStories.description: As the autopilot eval gate, I want the new probe registered and green with no new green-to-red regression so the change is safe to ship.
acceptanceCriteria: bash .claude/skills/eval/run.sh runs the suite; evals/RESULTS.md lists rl-delegation-write-worker as PASS

No NEW green-to-red regression — verified via the runner aggregate exit code ($? == 0), NOT by absence of REGRESSION rows in RESULTS.md; the pre-existing next-dev-prod REGRESSION (host-state probe, red on base) is expected and does not count
evals/RESULTS.md committed on the branch as a generated artifact

userStories.title: Changelog entry
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want the workflow-visible skill change recorded in CHANGELOG.md per context/rules/git.md.
acceptanceCriteria: CHANGELOG.md gains a one-line entry under '## [Unreleased]' in a '### Changed' (or '### Added') subsection, imperative mood, linking issue #57, noting the /delegate read-only sub-agent-type warning + guard probe
Entry committed in the same branch as the change
